data.body: DESIGN REFINEMENT (Joe, editability + de-risking): the batch step's group is a STATIC AUTHORED list of CRITERIA, each backed by a PROMPT-LIBRARY entry — the 'criteria library' mirrors the prompt library (1:1) so the same artifact wires into the eval seam (per-criterion eval = per-prompt eval) and the editor (add/remove/edit a criterion = its prompt). NOT a runtime-computed items list (which the visual editor cannot render/modify). Shape: batch: { prompt: <finder prompt-lib id>, criteria: [ {prompt: <criterion prompt-lib id>, when?: <overlay predicate>, with?: <inputs>} ], usd_budget?, model_ladder? }. The runner resolves each entry's when (reusing A1's overlay_triggers + predicate machinery), includes the truthy, packs the included criteria into budgeted batches under the shared finder, journals the plan (opaque to the interpreter). Reference runner (epic A): minimal-but-real — naive packing + per-batch finder call via AgentStepRunner + split-on-context-limit fallback; cost-escalation/shed are seam-supported but stubbed (epic B's production runner extracts sizing.py). This shape is editable (each criterion is a discrete authored node), mirrors the prompt library (eval+editing), and proves B's criteria-library wiring.
